briefing.title: Sell-Through Rate vs. Competitors
briefing.detail: Purse Maison sold 110 units MTD (6.8% sell-through on 1,615 inventory) vs. Miss Manila Luxe at 277 units (57.2% on 484) and The Bag Hub at 54 units (4.7% on 1,155) — our velocity is mid-pack but our avg ticket of ₱177,622 is 3rd highest, generating ₱25.88M in revenue, 2nd only to Miss Manila Luxe's ₱12.1M at volume.
briefing.action: Target 130 units sold by month-end by activating a flash promo on slow-moving Chanel Double Flap Jumbo (25 units live, deepest inventory in market) this week.

briefing.title: Hermès Birkin 30 — Undercut Risk
briefing.detail: Our lowest ask on Birkin 30 is ₱728,000 vs. market lowest at ₱575,000 — a ₱153,000 gap (26.6% premium) with 16 competing listings; Birkin 30 is a top seller across 3 competitor stores (The Orange Box, The Bag Hub, Shop with K).
briefing.action: Review the 5 live Birkin 30 units individually — reprice any non-exotic/standard leathers to ₱620,000–₱650,000 range to recapture mid-market buyers without racing to the bottom.

briefing.title: Kelly 28 — Significant Price Gap
briefing.detail: We hold 10 Kelly 28 units with a lowest ask of ₱735,000, while the market floor is ₱480,000 — a ₱255,000 (53%) premium across 28 total market listings, the most crowded Hermès model in the market.
briefing.action: Audit Kelly 28 stock by hardware/leather grade; reprice at least 3–4 entry-level units to ₱560,000–₱620,000 to stimulate movement and reduce the 10-unit concentration risk.

briefing.title: Mini Kelly — Overpriced Relative to Market Ceiling
briefing.detail: Our Mini Kelly lowest ask is ₱1,540,000 against a market highest ask of ₱1,680,000 and a market lowest of ₱970,000 — we are pricing near the ceiling with only 4 total listings in market, suggesting thin liquidity.
briefing.action: Hold pricing on Mini Kelly if pieces are exotic or rare hardware; if standard leather, test a reduction to ₱1,200,000–₱1,300,000 to close the ₱570,000 gap to market floor and convert one of the 2 units.

briefing.title: Chanel Double Flap Jumbo — Inventory Concentration
briefing.detail: 25 Jumbo units (our single largest hero model position) sit at our floor price of ₱188,000 — matched exactly to the market floor with 38 total competing listings, creating high substitution risk.
briefing.action: Bundle or feature 3–5 Jumbo units in a 'New Season Chanel Edit' campaign this week; differentiate by condition grade or card/box inclusion rather than competing purely on price.

briefing.title: Constance 24 — Price Gap vs. Market Floor
briefing.detail: Our Constance 24 lowest ask is ₱534,500 vs. market floor of ₱395,000 — a ₱139,500 (35%) gap across 16 listings, while Constance ranks in top sellers at Shop with K and A&J Luxury.
briefing.action: Reprice at least 2 of the 7 Constance 24 units to ₱450,000–₱480,000 to capture demand currently going to lower-priced competitors.

alerts: Miss Manila Luxe is an outlier velocity machine — 277 units sold MTD on only 484 inventory (57% sell-through) at avg ticket ₱43,726; they operate in a different mass/accessible luxury tier and are not a direct threat, but they are capturing high buyer frequency that could train customers away from premium stores.

Canone Bags Prime has a 3.9-day avg days-to-sell — fastest in the market — suggesting aggressive turnover pricing at avg ₱33,822; monitor if they begin listing Hermès or Chanel at disruptive price points.

Shop with K carries the highest avg ticket in the market at ₱332,922 (vs. our ₱177,622) with 27 units sold MTD — Hermès Constance and Hermès Other are strong movers for them, signaling an affluent buyer segment we should compete for more directly on Constance 18/24.

Zero aging90 inventory across ALL stores including Purse Maison — either market is genuinely liquid or stores are actively delisting/remarking before 90 days; our 13.5-day avg days-to-sell is healthy but monitor if Birkin 30 and Kelly 28 units start approaching 30+ days given pricing gaps.

Luxury Wish operates only 154 units but achieved ₱5.145M MTD at ₱350,065 avg ticket — highest revenue efficiency per SKU in the market; their Hermès HAC and Constance listings signal curated exotic inventory; potential sourcing competition for premium pieces.

pricing.suggestion: Our floor of ₱1,288,000 is only ₱8,000 (0.6%) above the market floor of ₱1,280,000 — pricing is competitive; hold current floor but ensure mid-tier units are spaced at ₱1,450,000–₱1,800,000 to preserve margin across the 7-unit position.

Reprice entry-level units from ₱728,000 down to ₱620,000–₱650,000 to close the 26.6% gap to the market floor of ₱575,000, reserving ₱575,000 only for heavily worn/no-card pieces we do not currently hold.

Our floor of ₱1,150,000 sits ₱300,000 (35%) above the market floor of ₱850,000 across 19 listings — reprice 2–3 standard-leather units to ₱950,000–₱1,000,000 to drive conversion without collapsing the overall position.

With 10 units and a ₱735,000 floor vs. ₱480,000 market floor, introduce a tiered pricing ladder: 2–3 units at ₱560,000–₱600,000 (entry), 4 units at ₱680,000–₱720,000 (mid), and 3 units at ₱750,000+ (premium leather/HW) to address all buyer segments.

Constance 18 floor of ₱695,000 is 6.9% above market floor (₱650,000) — acceptable; Constance 24 floor of ₱534,500 is 35% above market floor (₱395,000) — reduce 2 Constance 24 units to ₱450,000–₱480,000 immediately to compete with Shop with K and A&J Luxury where Constance is a proven top seller.

At ₱188,000 we match the market floor exactly across 38 competing listings — do not lower price further; instead differentiate through condition grading and accessory inclusion (dustbag, box, card) and consider pulling 3–5 weakest units from live listings temporarily to reduce our 25-unit market share exposure.
